title: Duplicate gateway is allowed for Podman while disallowed for docker
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**
I noticed that on RHEL7 with docker, duplicate gateway is not allowed by docker while creating macvlan
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/100113781/174931988-78b69fd3-bde8-40ab-aec9-81d76b204bcb.png)
However, there’s no this kind of restriction on RHEL8 with Podman 4.0. My question is "**whether reuse of gateway in macvlan interfaces is intentional behavior and not going to change in future?**"
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/100113781/174932113-ddb4ec8e-7d9d-4785-ac8a-d51fb23cc5f3.png)

**Output of `podman version`:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/100113781/174932180-655800df-ddb6-4b8e-82eb-bdd94f4e25ac.png)

comments.body:
For "bridge" network, Podman will check the uniqueness for subnet. Just wonder if Podman should check the uniqueness of subnet for "macvlan" network?
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/100113781/174938570-8cfe20fd-6443-4810-8d03-1e25b82ca11c.png)

Why should we prevent that? For me macvlan implies that you know what you are doing. Maclvan only assigns ips to the container in the network namespace. We cannot check for a unique subnet because we expect that the macvlan interface already exists with the same subnet on the host, so this check will always fail.

Bridge requires that check because having two interfaces with overlapping subnets will cause many routing issues.
Just because that the behavior is different with Docker. Just wanted to confirm that the current behavior on Podman is expected and will not be change in future.
We don't plan on changing it.
